To find the characteristic polynomial of the given matrix $A$, we need to compute the determinant of the matrix $A - \lambda I$, where $\lambda$ is a scalar variable and $I$ is the $4 \times 4$ identity matrix.

The matrix $A$ is:
$$
A = \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 \frac{1}{2} & -\frac{101}{8} & -\frac{17}{8} & -\frac{47}{4} \\
 -\frac{75}{8} & -\frac{109}{8} & \frac{111}{8} & -\frac{9}{4} \\
 \frac{27}{8} & \frac{19}{8} & -\frac{21}{2} & -\frac{3}{4} \\
 \frac{35}{4} & \frac{13}{8} & -\frac{21}{2} & \frac{25}{8}
\end{array}\right)
$$

To simplify the calculation, let's first clear the denominators by factoring out $\frac{1}{8}$ from the entire matrix.
$$
A = \frac{1}{8} \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 4 & -101 & -17 & -94 \\
 -75 & -109 & 111 & -18 \\
 27 & 19 & -84 & -6 \\
 70 & 13 & -84 & 25
\end{array}\right)
$$
Let $B = 8A$. Then the entries of $B$ are integers.
$$
B = \left(\begin{array}{cccc}
 4 & -101 & -17 & -94 \\
 -75 & -109 & 111 & -18 \\
 27 & 19 & -84 & -6 \\
 70 & 13 & -84 & 25
\end{array}\right)
$$
The characteristic polynomial of $A$ is $p_A(\lambda) = \det(A - \lambda I)$.
